title: DOOM: The Dark Ages

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT (radeonsi, navi21, LLVM 18.1.8, DRM 3.59, 6.12.4-1-MANJARO)
notes: No matter what fix i found on the internet for it, (i.e. different proton versions, reinstalling the game, different launch optition combos, etc..) every time I launch doom:tda I could only get a black screen after the intro vid. It would only played the menu music yet the menu never appeared. This isn't like the menu just wasn't rendering, no, the menu didn't exist, no buttons, no menu optition, nothing. Unfortunately that's not where my issues end with the game, upon trying to forceably close the game it would then hard lock my system, with it sometimes recovering and sometimes not. At the moment it does seem like it might just be Manjaro Linux (or just my system) having problems running this game, so if you're on manjaro just be wary of this, it might not work for you.

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (radeonsi, gfx1201, ACO, DRM 3.64, 6.19.2-pikaos)
notes:
The game freezes on the title screen when I press the space button in order to get to the main menu. I tried both Proton, GE-Proton with no luck. I also tried running it on my steam deck oled + win10 on dualboot and both flawlessly worked, so at this point I thought it was either a Proton or CPU issue.

As it is now, it turns out (based on this post: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8690#issuecomment-3757614309 + 4 other proton db review with the same issues and using intel alder lake cpu's without e-cores, that the game just freezes at the title screen if you dont have e-cores in your intel alder lake cpu. The fact that this is still not patched is incredibly annoying

So if you are in this situation your choices are either using windows, an intel cpu with e-cores or an amd cpu.

Very niche and annoying problem, still not solved.
title: DOOM: The Dark Ages
